body.title: Palace: C-agent-taxonomy concept node
body.result: palace.concepts.agent-taxonomy node created with three-class definitions + Librarian annotation; C-agent-taxonomy.md stub added to lab/design_docs/decisions/
body.description:
Create palace concept node C-agent-taxonomy defining the three formal agent classes used in UU. This is shared vocabulary — when Akien or CC say 'utility agent' there's a canonical definition to point at.

Three classes:
- **utility** — single bounded capability, composable, called like a function. Low autonomy, high reliability within scope. Examples: Scraps, GoogleSecretary.
- **specialized** — domain workflow expert. Knows its lane deeply, orchestrates multi-step work within it, doesn't generalize. Examples: Granny (dispatch), Nanny.
- **general** — broad reasoning, novel problem-solving, high context. Examples: Igor, CC, OpenClaw-style agents.

Librarian note in concept: wider scope than a typical utility agent but not autonomous — classify as utility with a 'platform utility' annotation.

**Affected files:** lab/design_docs/decisions/C-agent-taxonomy.md (new concept stub); adc.palace INSERT via psql

**Design rules:** palace-first — concept lives in DB + .md stub; no code changes in this ticket

**Scope boundary:** IN — concept node with three-class definitions + Librarian annotation. OUT — registry field changes (T-device-registry-agent-class); routing changes based on class.

**Completion criteria:** (1) `SELECT path FROM adc.palace WHERE path = 'palace.concepts.agent-taxonomy'` returns one row; (2) concept content includes all three class definitions; (3) C-agent-taxonomy.md stub exists in lab/design_docs/
